payload.title: MTA Advertising Policy

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA") had long maintained an Advertising Policy to set standards for the display of advertising in and on the facilities, vehicles and other property (together "Property") of the MTA and its affiliated and subsidiary agencies (together "MTA").

tool_args.label: Jon Kaufman, Chief Strategic Initiatives
tool_args.raw_text: Jon Kaufman, Chief Strategic Initiatives

tool_args.label: Jessica Lazarus, Deputy Chief, Commercial Ventures
tool_args.raw_text: Jessica Lazarus, Deputy Chief, Commercial Ventures

tool_args.label: Finance Committee Meeting - MTA Advertising Policy
tool_args.raw_text: Finance Committee 6/23/2025

payload.description: Finance Committee meeting to consider updates to the MTA Advertising Policy.

tool_args.label: MTA Board Meeting - Advertising Policy Approval

payload.description: Board meeting to approve and adopt the updated MTA Advertising Policy.

tool_args.label: Audio Announcements and Advertising Pilots Renewed
tool_args.raw_text: the existing pilots for audio announcements and audio advertising are being renewed until July 2026

payload.description: Existing pilots for audio announcements and audio advertising renewed until July 2026.

tool_args.label: MTA Advertising Policy Effective Date
tool_args.raw_text: This Advertising Policy is effective as of June __, 2025.

payload.description: Effective date of the updated MTA Advertising Policy, to be set by the Board.

tool_args.label: Policy Scope Clarification
tool_args.raw_text: First, the updated policy clarifies the scope of the MTA's Advertising Policy applies to all MTA properties, including station advertising, billboards, and digital properties.

payload.claim_text: The updated policy clarifies the scope of the MTA's Advertising Policy applies to all MTA properties, including station advertising, billboards, and digital properties.
payload.subject: MTA Advertising Policy scope

tool_args.label: Large Format Advertising Restrictions
tool_args.raw_text: Second, the updated policy clarifies the requirements for proposed "Large Format Advertising." This format includes (i) advertising that physically "wraps" and so covers all or nearly all of the exterior surfaces of subway cars, train cars, or buses and (ii) any other advertisement in any medium that appears on MTA Property that is an equivalent or greater size. Any advertising which prominently displays the unclothed human body, regardless of the nature of any clothing items depicted, will not be accepted in such formats.

payload.claim_text: Large Format Advertising includes (i) advertising that physically wraps exterior surfaces of subway cars, train cars, or buses and (ii) any other equivalent or greater sized advertisement. Advertisements prominently displaying the unclothed human body will not be accepted in such formats.
payload.subject: Large Format Advertising

tool_args.label: Alcohol Advertising Limited Allowance
tool_args.raw_text: Third, the updated policy maintains alcohol advertising in the policy's "Prohibited" category, while also now allowing for alcohol advertising in the following limited settings: - On digital displays within stations or attached to station exteriors, and on the interior of railcars and buses, but only outside of typical student commutation hours (6-8 a.m. and 2-5 p.m. on weekdays when school is in session); - On static displays inside (1) major commuter rail hubs and (2) stations adjacent to large venues where alcohol is lawfully sold; - In "train wrap" format on the S shuttle train between Times Square and Grand Central – but not on the exterior of any other MTA railcars or buses; and - On billboards, but only where the advertiser certifies that such billboards are not readable within 500 feet of a school, playground, or place of worship.

payload.claim_text: Alcohol advertising remains prohibited category but is now allowed in limited settings: digital displays outside student commutation hours; static displays in major commuter rail hubs and stations near large venues; train wrap on S shuttle only; and billboards with 500-foot certification.
payload.subject: alcohol advertising

tool_args.label: Final Determination Reassignment to Chief Administrative Officer
tool_args.raw_text: Fourth, the updated advertising policy reassigns within the MTA the responsibility to issue final determinations regarding decisions by the MTA's Advertising Committee. Advertisers can request a final determination after the committee rejects an ad pursuant to the Advertising Policy. Such final determinations will now be made by the MTA's Chief Administrative Officer or their designee.

payload.claim_text: Final determinations regarding Advertising Committee decisions will now be made by the MTA's Chief Administrative Officer or their designee, reassigned from previous authority.
payload.subject: final determination authority

tool_args.label: Administrative and Textual Clean Up Changes
tool_args.raw_text: Fifth, the proposal before the Board also includes several other changes that are administrative and textual clean up. These changes would not materially alter the types of advertising that are displayed in the MTA system.

payload.claim_text: The proposal includes several administrative and textual clean up changes that would not materially alter the types of advertising displayed in the MTA system.
payload.subject: administrative clean up changes

tool_args.label: MTA Finance Committee considered Advertising Policy update
tool_args.raw_text: Finance Committee 6/23/2025 X 1 CFO

tool_args.label: MTA Board approved Advertising Policy update
MTA Board
Board approval recommendation for Advertising Policy
tool_args.raw_text: It is recommended that the Board approve and adopt the annexed updated MTA Advertising Policy.

payload.description: The Board is recommended to approve and adopt the updated MTA Advertising Policy at its June 25, 2025 meeting.

tool_args.label: Alcohol Advertising - Digital Display Time Restrictions
tool_args.raw_text: Alcohol advertisements may only be displayed: a. in digital formats within the interior of MTA stations, railcars or buses, or affixed to station exteriors, provided that the advertisements: i. may only run outside of typical student commutation hours, i.e., 6 a.m. to 8 a.m. and 2 p.m. to 5 p.m. on weekdays when school is in session

payload.claim_text: Alcohol advertisements on digital displays within stations, railcars, buses, or station exteriors may only run outside of typical student commutation hours (6-8 a.m. and 2-5 p.m. on weekdays when school is in session).
payload.subject: alcohol advertising restrictions

tool_args.label: Alcohol Advertising - S Shuttle Train Wrap Only
tool_args.raw_text: in fixed "train wrap" format only on the S subway line (i.e., the shuttle between Times Square and Grand Central)

payload.claim_text: Alcohol advertisements in train wrap format are only permitted on the S subway line (shuttle between Times Square and Grand Central), not on the exterior of any other MTA railcars or buses.
payload.subject: alcohol advertising restrictions

tool_args.label: Alcohol Advertising - 500-Foot School Certification
tool_args.raw_text: on billboards, subject to the advertiser certifying to the MTA or its advertising vendor that the advertisements may not be readable within 500 feet of a school, playground, or place of worship

payload.claim_text: Alcohol advertisements on billboards and station exteriors require advertiser certification that such ads are not readable within 500 feet of a school, playground, or place of worship.
payload.subject: alcohol advertising restrictions

tool_args.label: Large Format Advertising - No Lingerie, Underwear, Swimsuits
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA will not accept Large Format Advertising that depicts or explicitly references lingerie, underwear, or swimsuits; prominently displays the unclothed human body, regardless of the nature of any clothing items depicted; or promotes any brand that prominently promotes itself in its marketing materials as a company selling Sexually Oriented Products or Services.

payload.claim_text: Large Format Advertising that depicts or references lingerie, underwear, swimsuits, unclothed human body, or brands selling Sexually Oriented Products or Services will not be accepted.
payload.subject: Large Format Advertising restrictions

tool_args.label: MTA Advertising Space as Non-Public Forum
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA expressly intends that the advertising space on the Property be a non-public forum.

payload.claim_text: The MTA expressly intends that the advertising space on its Property be a non-public forum.
payload.subject: legal status of advertising space

tool_args.label: Final Advertising Determinations by Chief Administrative Officer
tool_args.raw_text: If the advertiser has received notice that the Advertising Review Committee has determined that a proposed advertisement does not comply with the Advertising Policy, the advertiser may request a final determination from the MTA Chief Administrative Officer or their designee.

payload.claim_text: Advertisers may request a final determination from the MTA Chief Administrative Officer or their designee after the Advertising Review Committee rejects an advertisement.
payload.subject: advertising appeal process
